linting.notes: Primary linting is via pre-commit hooks. The nox lint session runs pre-commit and optionally mypy. Ruff handles both linting and formatting. All lint checks passed successfully during validation.

setup_required: Requires specific vLLM version (built from source for CPU testing)
testing.mock_patterns: Uses pytest fixtures for server setup, mocking handled via pytest-mock
testing.pytest_options: -ra -k "not hf_data" (default, skips HuggingFace data download tests)

markers.hf_data: marks tests that download data from HF hub (deselect with '-m "not hf_data"')

testing.notes: Tests require specific vLLM version compatibility. The adapter is tightly coupled to vLLM's API. CI builds vLLM from source for versions v0.10.0, v0.10.1.1, v0.10.2, v0.11.0, v0.11.2. Tests involve starting actual gRPC and HTTP servers with vLLM engine. conftest.py provides fixtures for server setup and ports.

vllm_build_steps: Check out vllm repository at specific ref
Set VLLM_VERSION_OVERRIDE to built wheel path
ci.notes: CI runs tests against multiple vLLM versions. Each test run requires building vLLM from source with CPU support. Uses uv for dependency management and nox for session management. Caches include nox envs, ccache, and HuggingFace hub.

custom_build_steps: Protobuf generation via grpc_tools.protoc (runs during build_py)
build.notes: Uses setuptools_scm for version management. Custom build command generates gRPC/protobuf files. setup.py defines BuildPyAndGenerateGrpc class extending build_py.

conventions.test_naming: Functions prefixed with test_*, pytest-style fixtures in conftest.py
conventions.import_style: Absolute imports, isort configured via ruff with known-first-party=['vllm_tgis_adapter']
conventions.code_style: Ruff formatting, comprehensive linting rules (select ALL with specific ignores)
conventions.type_checking: mypy enabled for src and tests, check_untyped_defs=false, allows missing imports for grpc
conventions.notes: Project uses ruff for both linting and formatting. Type checking is optional but configured. Tests use pytest fixtures extensively for server setup. Pre-commit hooks enforce formatting and style.

lint_commands.notes: All hooks passed: ruff, ruff-format, codespell, pre-commit-hooks, pyupgrade, TOML/YAML formatters
Type checking passed with some import-untyped warnings (grpc modules)
Runs pre-commit in isolated nox environment, all checks passed

test_commands.notes: ImportError: cannot import name 'maybe_register_tokenizer_info_endpoint' from vLLM. Tests require specific vLLM version built from source. Standard pip-installed vLLM (0.18.0) incompatible with adapter code expecting vLLM 0.10.x-0.11.x API.
Would have same ImportError. Requires VLLM_VERSION_OVERRIDE pointing to compatible wheel built from source.

container_recipe.notes: Linting works perfectly in a standard container. Testing requires building vLLM from source at specific versions (see CI workflow for exact process). The adapter is tightly coupled to vLLM API versions. For full test validation, must build vLLM from source following .github/workflows/tests.yaml steps.

results.notes: Nox lint session (running pre-commit in isolated env) passed
Tests failed to run due to vLLM API incompatibility. The pip-installed vLLM 0.18.0 has different API than what the adapter expects (vLLM 0.10.x-0.11.x).

validation.summary: Linting: ✅ ALL PASSED (pre-commit, ruff, mypy, nox). Testing: ❌ FAILED - requires specific vLLM version built from source. Standard pip install gets vLLM 0.18.0 which is incompatible with adapter code expecting 0.10.x-0.11.x API.

gaps: Tests cannot run with standard pip-installed vLLM - require specific version built from source
The adapter is tightly coupled to specific vLLM API versions (v0.10.0 through v0.11.2)
CI builds vLLM from source with CPU support - complex setup not easily reproducible
Tests require actual vLLM engine initialization which needs model files or mocking
No simple way to run tests without building matching vLLM version
Tests involve starting actual servers (gRPC/HTTP) and require significant resource setup
HuggingFace hub tests download model data (skipped by default with -k 'not hf_data')
No documented minimum/maximum vLLM version compatibility range
agent_readiness_justification: Linting is fully functional and validated (high confidence). Testing requires complex vLLM build from source which is well-documented in CI but not easily automated. An agent can validate lint changes reliably but cannot validate test changes without building specific vLLM versions.
